g.text: Join the line at the registrar's table.
Thank her, and prove the bar alone.
Face the section on its worst day.
Say less, and re-check the facts yoursel...
Stand with the section at assembly.
Decide what to do with what you found.
Carry the term to its last Friday.
Stay, and walk him to the truth.
Decide what the ache is telling you.
Hold steady through the accusation week.
Take your seat in the audience.
Keep it for the spring recital.
Go where the repair is happening.
Take your desk for the concert.
Carry the true bar into ensemble week.
Decide what the thread is worth.
Take the riddle back to your room.
Ask {TEACHER_B} straight out what the ri...
Sit with the riddle another week.
Report to the workshop as copyist.
Come in on the frost Monday.
Sit with the studio in the wreckage.
Give December to the middle movement.
Stand by while the truth walks.
Come to the last week of term.
Take your seat for the concert.
Finish the term with open eyes.
Sit in the studio circle after.
Speak to why he did it.
Let the term mend toward December.
Come in on the frost Monday.
Hold the studio together that week.
Let the adults carry it, and write.
Ask that it go unawarded this term.
Take your seat for the concert.
Finish the movement with the studio.
Gather with the choir at noon.
Help {TEACHER_C} answer the memo with fa...
Rally the choir to re-mark scores overni...
Be in the office when the board writes b...
Urge {TEACHER_C} to sign and protect the...
Urge her to wait for the infirmary log.
Let it fade into the term's noise.
Sing the concert with the letter sent.
Hold the verse to the end.
Work beside him till the urn runs dry.
Ask him, kindly, how he knows the markin...
Say nothing and finish the scores.
Hold the door open through the week.
Bring the scores home to December.
Sing the concert with clear eyes.
Meet with the choir that evening.
Weigh the compromise with the choir.
Let the term turn under you.
Ask to sing it as a duet with {HEAD_CHOR...
